title: limonium xerophilum
intro: Limonium xerophilum, also known as sea lavender or marsh rosemary, is a perennial, succulent plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, Limonium xerophilum has been prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and calmness.
